pages.full_text:
Kler + Nana gave us supper in the Swan Hotel. Very good. Conrad has been behaving very well.
	Sunday we packed up + left to watch at the Glad Mountain Gliding Club. We saw about 3 planes take off, [?spun] Fantastic vviews + [?ridge]. ([?we forgot [illegible]to])
	On for fish + chips in Abergavenny. On to Raglan Castle. Lovely sunshine.
	Then on to home.
__Tuesday [?7/6]th August__
	10 we left home + dropped Tinder off at kennels. The trafic + cars in the waiting area at the Channel Tunnel were lighter than usual.
__Wednesday 7th August__
	We camped next to a lake in a residential camp site, without paying at [?Humancourt] ** between [?Arras] + Cambrai.
	We walked in the woods in the evening.
	The lake was stipled with rain while we had breakfast + it hasn't stopped
pages.notes: Top of page continues from previous spread. 'Glad Mountain Gliding Club' may be 'Black Mountain Gliding Club' — the 'Gl' could be 'Bl' (faint). 'Humancourt' is uncertain; annotation reads 'between [?Arras] + Cambrai' added as interlinear note in smaller script. 'Tuesday [?7/6]th August' — the digit is ambiguous, possibly a correction from 7 to 6 or vice versa; if Tuesday=6th and Wednesday=7th August, year 2013 fits (Wednesday 7 Aug 2013). Year is uncertain — 0.5 confidence. 'Kler' may be a name or abbreviation. 'Conrad' appears to be a person (child). 'Tinder' is likely a pet (dog at kennels).

pages.full_text:
raining during our trip on to St. Quentin.
	We visited the wrong big church first + then found the Basilica. Its onomous + vertical.

[Photo: labyrinth floor mosaic]

__Labyrinthe at St Quentin__

__Picardie__
__Catthedrals__

[Small map showing: Amiens, Beauvais, Noyon, Senlis, Laon, St Quentin, Soissons]
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 37 visible at top-right corner. Page contains a large glued-in photograph of a labyrinth/maze floor tile pattern (octagonal, black and white/gold tiles). Below that is a small printed/glued map of Picardie region showing cathedral towns including Amiens, Beauvais, Noyon, Senlis, Laon, St Quentin, Soissons with wheat-sheaf motif. 'onomous' likely means 'enormous' — writer omitted 'en'. 'Catthedrals' is spelled with double-t in diary. Text at top of page continues from left page (same trip, day continuing).
